_description: Schema v2 fixture for the WT-03 additive `siblingGroup` field on `dispatchable-sessions` peer objects. Two PODS workers share a `siblingGroup` (the PODS session ID) but live in different `workDir` values — the worktree-mode case where the conventional workDir-based grouping would fail to identify them as siblings. v1 readers ignore the new field; v2 readers consume it for sibling discovery in `/0-uldf-pods-collab-sync` and `/0-uldf-dispatch`.

_lineage.schemaContext: Frozen DISPATCH-01 schema extended additively at commit (this Arc 1 commit). v1 fixtures (e.g., csi-01-registration-fixture.json scenarios) MUST continue to validate against the unchanged base shape — `siblingGroup` is purely optional.

scenarios.name: two-pods-workers-same-sibling-group-different-workdirs
scenarios.description: Two PODS workers (sessionId `WORKER-A`, `WORKER-B`) registered with `siblingGroup: pods-20260510-XXXXXX`. Worker A's workDir is the anchor repo; Worker B's workDir is a sibling worktree. The dispatchable-sessions oracle MUST emit both peers with the same `siblingGroup` value, regardless of workDir divergence.
invariants: expectedOracleOutput.peers[0].workDir != expectedOracleOutput.peers[1].workDir (workdir divergence is the WT-03 motivating case)
Both peers retain all v1 fields (sessionId, sessionRole, role, workDir, claudeShellPid, dispatchable, spawnedAt) — siblingGroup is strictly additive.

scenarios.name: non-pods-worker-no-sibling-group
scenarios.description: A non-PODS interactive worker (no `siblingGroup` in registry entry). Oracle output MUST omit the `siblingGroup` key entirely (NOT emit it as null) — the additive contract requires absence rather than null. v1 consumers can continue to assume the key is absent.
invariants: expectedOracleOutput.peers[0] does NOT contain key 'siblingGroup' (absence, not null) — frozen-schema additive contract
v1 fields present unchanged (sessionId, sessionRole, role, workDir, claudeShellPid, dispatchable, spawnedAt)

matchSyntax._doc: Same conventions as csi-01-registration-fixture.json: fields whose values begin with '<MATCH:' are matched as Python regex; literal values matched literally; absence assertions live in the invariants block.

pairedOracle.name: dispatchable-sessions
pairedOracle._doc: Verification path: build a sandbox active-sessions.json populated with input.registrySessions, plus an alive-PID rewrite (smoke harness substitutes real PIDs); invoke `bash run.sh`; assert expectedOracleOutput shape (additive siblingGroup present in scenario 1, absent in scenario 2). The csi-01-smoke harness extension in Phase J of Arc 1 wires this fixture into the regression set.
